API Reference¶

Core Modules¶

Core Bitcoin primitives.

Bitcoin Script primitives.

Provides opcode constants, the CScript byte-sequence wrapper, and helpers and builders for standard script types.

class ordex.core.script.CScript(data: bytes = b'')[source]¶

A Bitcoin script — a sequence of opcodes and push-data operations.

Inherits from bytes so it can be used directly as a byte string.

classmethod from_ops(*ops) → CScript[source]¶

Build a script from a sequence of opcodes and data pushes.

Each element is either an int (opcode) or bytes (data push).

get_p2pkh_hash() → bytes | None[source]¶

Extract the 20-byte pubkey hash from a P2PKH script.

get_p2sh_hash() → bytes | None[source]¶

Extract the 20-byte script hash from a P2SH script.

is_p2pkh() → bool[source]¶

OP_DUP OP_HASH160 <20 bytes> OP_EQUALVERIFY OP_CHECKSIG

is_p2sh() → bool[source]¶

OP_HASH160 <20 bytes> OP_EQUAL

is_unspendable() → bool[source]¶

Script starts with OP_RETURN.

is_witness_v0_keyhash() → bool[source]¶

OP_0 <20 bytes>

is_witness_v0_scripthash() → bool[source]¶

OP_0 <32 bytes>

is_witness_v1_taproot() → bool[source]¶

OP_1 <32 bytes>

classmethod op_return(data: bytes) → CScript[source]¶

Create an OP_RETURN script for embedding data.

classmethod p2pkh(pubkey_hash: bytes) → CScript[source]¶

Create a P2PKH script: OP_DUP OP_HASH160 <hash> OP_EQUALVERIFY OP_CHECKSIG.

classmethod p2sh(script_hash: bytes) → CScript[source]¶

Create a P2SH script: OP_HASH160 <hash> OP_EQUAL.

classmethod p2wpkh(pubkey_hash: bytes) → CScript[source]¶

Create a P2WPKH script: OP_0 <20-byte hash>.

classmethod p2wsh(script_hash: bytes) → CScript[source]¶

Create a P2WSH script: OP_0 <32-byte hash>.

class ordex.core.script.CScriptNum[source]¶

Encode an integer in Bitcoin’s script number format (CScriptNum).

exception ordex.core.script.ScriptError[source]¶

Exception raised during script validation.

class ordex.core.script.ScriptInterpreter[source]¶

Bitcoin script interpreter for basic script validation.

Supports P2PKH, P2WPKH, P2SH, and bare script validation.

evaluate(script: CScript, witness: List[bytes] | None = None) → bool[source]¶

Evaluate a script with optional witness data (for SegWit).

reset() → None[source]¶

Reset the interpreter state.

verify_p2pkh(pubkey_hash: bytes, signature: bytes, pubkey: bytes) → bool[source]¶

Verify a P2PKH script.

Parameters:

  • pubkey_hash – 20-byte hash from the script

  • signature – DER-encoded signature

  • pubkey – Public key

Returns:

True if the script validates

verify_p2sh(script_hash: bytes, redeem_script: CScript, signature: bytes, pubkey: bytes) → bool[source]¶

Verify a P2SH script.

Parameters:

  • script_hash – 20-byte hash from the P2SH script

  • redeem_script – The original redeem script

  • signature – Signature

  • pubkey – Public key

Returns:

True if the script validates

verify_p2wpkh(pubkey_hash: bytes, signature: bytes, pubkey: bytes) → bool[source]¶

Verify a P2WPKH (SegWit) script.

Parameters:

  • pubkey_hash – 20-byte hash from the witness program

  • signature – DER-encoded signature

  • pubkey – Public key

Returns:

True if the script validates

ordex.core.script.verify_script(script: CScript, signature: bytes | None = None, pubkey: bytes | None = None, witness: List[bytes] | None = None) → bool[source]¶

Verify a script.

Parameters:

  • script – The script to verify

  • signature – Optional signature (for P2PKH/P2SH)

  • pubkey – Optional public key

  • witness – Optional witness data (for SegWit)

Returns:

True if the script validates

Cryptographic hash functions used by OrdexCoin and OrdexGold.

  • sha256d: double SHA-256 (OXC PoW, both chains for txid/block-id)

  • hash160: SHA-256 + RIPEMD-160 (address derivation)

  • scrypt_hash: Scrypt(N=1024, r=1, p=1, dklen=32) (OXG PoW)

ordex.core.hash.hash160(data: bytes) → bytes[source]¶

RIPEMD-160(SHA-256(data)) — used for P2PKH / P2SH address derivation.

ordex.core.hash.merkle_hash(left: bytes, right: bytes) → bytes[source]¶

Hash two Merkle tree nodes together with double-SHA-256.

ordex.core.hash.scrypt_hash(header_bytes: bytes) → bytes[source]¶

Scrypt hash used for OrdexGold PoW.

Parameters match Litecoin: N=1024, r=1, p=1, dklen=32. The input is the serialized 80-byte block header.

ordex.core.hash.sha256(data: bytes) → bytes[source]¶

Single SHA-256 hash.

ordex.core.hash.sha256d(data: bytes) → bytes[source]¶

Double SHA-256 hash (SHA-256 of SHA-256).

This is the standard hash used for Bitcoin/OrdexCoin block headers, transaction IDs, and Merkle trees.

ECDSA key handling for secp256k1.

Provides private/public key generation, WIF encoding/decoding, and public key compression. Uses the ecdsa library.

class ordex.core.key.PrivateKey(secret: bytes)[source]¶

A secp256k1 private key (32-byte scalar).

classmethod from_wif(wif: str) → PrivateKey[source]¶

Decode a WIF-encoded private key.

classmethod generate() → PrivateKey[source]¶

Generate a new random private key.

public_key(compressed: bool = True) → PublicKey[source]¶

Derive the corresponding public key.

property secret: bytes¶

Raw 32-byte secret scalar.

sign(message_hash: bytes) → bytes[source]¶

Sign a 32-byte hash, returning a DER-encoded signature.

to_wif(secret_key_prefix: int = 203, compressed: bool = True) → str[source]¶

Encode as WIF string.

Parameters:

  • secret_key_prefix – The chain-specific SECRET_KEY byte (OXC mainnet=203/0xCB, OXG mainnet=166/0xA6).

  • compressed – Whether to flag the key as compressed.

class ordex.core.key.PublicKey(data: bytes)[source]¶

A secp256k1 public key (compressed or uncompressed).

property data: bytes¶

Raw serialized public key bytes.

classmethod from_point(x: int, y: int, compressed: bool = True) → PublicKey[source]¶

Create from curve point coordinates.

hash160() → bytes[source]¶

RIPEMD-160(SHA-256(pubkey)) — the 20-byte pubkey hash.

Base58Check and Bech32/Bech32m encoding/decoding.

Used for generating human-readable OrdexCoin and OrdexGold addresses.

ordex.core.base58.b58check_decode(s: str) → Tuple[bytes, bytes][source]¶

Decode Base58Check, returning (version_byte, payload).

Raises ValueError on checksum mismatch.

ordex.core.base58.b58check_encode(version: bytes, payload: bytes) → str[source]¶

Encode with Base58Check (version + payload + 4-byte checksum).

ordex.core.base58.b58decode(s: str) → bytes[source]¶

Decode a Base58 string to bytes.

ordex.core.base58.b58encode(data: bytes) → str[source]¶

Encode bytes to a Base58 string.

ordex.core.base58.bech32_decode(bech: str) → Tuple[str, int, bytes][source]¶

Decode a Bech32/Bech32m address.

Returns (hrp, witness_version, witness_program). Raises ValueError on invalid encoding.

ordex.core.base58.bech32_encode(hrp: str, witver: int, witprog: bytes) → str[source]¶

Encode a witness program as a Bech32/Bech32m address.

witver 0 uses Bech32 (BIP173), witver 1+ uses Bech32m (BIP350).

Binary serialization primitives matching the Bitcoin/Ordex wire format.

Provides CompactSize (varint) encoding, little-endian integer I/O, and vector/string serialization helpers.

ordex.core.serialize.from_bytes(data: bytes, read_fn: Callable[[BinaryIO], T]) → T[source]¶

Deserialize an object from bytes via a read callback.

ordex.core.serialize.read_bytes(f: BinaryIO, n: int) → bytes[source]¶

Read exactly n bytes from f.

ordex.core.serialize.read_compact_size(f: BinaryIO) → int[source]¶

Read a CompactSize-encoded unsigned integer from a stream.

ordex.core.serialize.read_hash256(f: BinaryIO) → bytes[source]¶

Read a 256-bit hash (32 bytes, internal byte order).

ordex.core.serialize.read_string(f: BinaryIO) → bytes[source]¶

Read a CompactSize-length-prefixed byte string.

ordex.core.serialize.read_vector(f: BinaryIO, deserialize_fn: Callable[[BinaryIO], T]) → List[T][source]¶

Read a vector: CompactSize count followed by count elements.

ordex.core.serialize.to_bytes(write_fn: Callable[[BinaryIO], None]) → bytes[source]¶

Serialize an object to bytes via a write callback.

ordex.core.serialize.write_compact_size(f: BinaryIO, n: int) → None[source]¶

Write a CompactSize-encoded unsigned integer to a stream.

ordex.core.serialize.write_hash256(f: BinaryIO, h: bytes) → None[source]¶

Write a 256-bit hash (32 bytes).

ordex.core.serialize.write_string(f: BinaryIO, data: bytes) → None[source]¶

Write a CompactSize-length-prefixed byte string.

ordex.core.serialize.write_vector(f: BinaryIO, items: List[T], serialize_fn: Callable[[BinaryIO, T], None]) → None[source]¶

Write a vector: CompactSize count followed by serialized elements.

256-bit unsigned integer type for block hashes, proof-of-work targets, etc.

Mirrors Bitcoin Core’s arith_uint256 / uint256 types. The internal representation is a Python int (arbitrary precision), but all serialization is 32-byte little-endian.

class ordex.core.uint256.Uint256(value: int = 0)[source]¶

Immutable 256-bit unsigned integer.

bits() → int[source]¶

Return the position of the highest set bit (1-indexed), or 0.

classmethod from_bytes_le(data: bytes) → Uint256[source]¶

Create from 32-byte little-endian representation.

classmethod from_compact(compact: int) → Uint256[source]¶

Decode the nBits compact representation into a full 256-bit target.

Format: the top byte is the number of bytes (exponent), and the lower 3 bytes are the mantissa. Negative flag is the MSB of the mantissa.

classmethod from_hex(hex_str: str) → Uint256[source]¶

Create from a hex string (big-endian, like block hashes are displayed).

get_compact() → int[source]¶

Encode as the nBits compact representation.

to_bytes_le() → bytes[source]¶

Return the 32-byte little-endian representation.

to_hex() → str[source]¶

Return the big-endian hex string (like block hash display).

Wallet Modules¶

Wallet and address management.

BIP32 HD Wallet implementation for OrdexCoin and OrdexGold.

Provides hierarchical deterministic key derivation as per BIP32.

class ordex.wallet.hd.BIP32Path(path: str = 'm')[source]¶

Represents a BIP32 derivation path.

class ordex.wallet.hd.ExtendedKey(key: bytes, chain_code: bytes, version: int, depth: int = 0, parent_fingerprint: int = 0, child_index: int = 0, is_private: bool = True)[source]¶

An extended key (private or public) for BIP32.

derive(index: int, hardened: bool = False) → ExtendedKey[source]¶

Derive a child key at the given index.

Parameters:

  • index – Child index (0-2^31-1 for normal, 2^31-2^32-1 for hardened)

  • hardened – Whether to use hardened derivation

Returns:

New ExtendedKey instance

derive_path(path: str) → ExtendedKey[source]¶

Derive a key from a path like m/44’/0’/0’/0/0

property fingerprint: int¶

Get the fingerprint of this key.

classmethod from_base58(encoded: str) → ExtendedKey[source]¶

Deserialize an extended key from Base58Check format.

private_key() → PrivateKey[source]¶

Get the PrivateKey object from this extended key.

public_key() → PublicKey[source]¶

Get the PublicKey object from this extended key.

to_base58() → str[source]¶

Serialize the extended key to Base58Check format.

class ordex.wallet.hd.HDWallet(root_key: ExtendedKey, params)[source]¶

HD Wallet for BIP32 key derivation.

derive_account(account: int, purpose: int = 44) → ExtendedKey[source]¶

Derive an account key (e.g., m/44’/0’/0’).

For OrdexCoin (OXC): purpose=44, coin_type=0 For OrdexGold (OXG): purpose=44, coin_type=1

Parameters:

  • account – Account index

  • purpose – BIP purpose (default: 44 for BIP44)

Returns:

ExtendedKey for the account

derive_address(chain_key: ExtendedKey, index: int) → dict[source]¶

Derive an address at the given index.

Parameters:

  • chain_key – Chain extended key (external or internal)

  • index – Address index

Returns:

Dict with privkey, pubkey, address info

derive_external_chain(account_key: ExtendedKey) → ExtendedKey[source]¶

Derive the external chain (receive addresses).

Parameters:

account_key – Account extended key

Returns:

ExtendedKey for external chain

derive_internal_chain(account_key: ExtendedKey) → ExtendedKey[source]¶

Derive the internal chain (change addresses).

Parameters:

account_key – Account extended key

Returns:

ExtendedKey for internal chain

classmethod from_extended_key(extended_key: str) → HDWallet[source]¶

Create HD wallet from an extended key string.

Parameters:

extended_key – Base58 encoded extended private key

Returns:

HDWallet instance

classmethod from_mnemonic(params, mnemonic: str, passphrase: str = '') → HDWallet[source]¶

Create HD wallet from BIP39 mnemonic.

Parameters:

  • params – ChainParams for the network

  • mnemonic – BIP39 mnemonic phrase

  • passphrase – Optional passphrase for PBKDF2

Returns:

HDWallet instance

classmethod generate(params, seed: bytes | None = None) → HDWallet[source]¶

Generate a new HD wallet from random seed or specified seed.

Parameters:

  • params – ChainParams for the network

  • seed – Optional 64-byte seed (if None, generates random)

Returns:

HDWallet instance

get_change_addresses(account: int = 0, count: int = 20) → list[source]¶

Get a list of change addresses.

Parameters:

  • account – Account index

  • count – Number of addresses to generate

Returns:

List of address dicts

get_receiving_addresses(account: int = 0, count: int = 20) → list[source]¶

Get a list of receiving addresses.

Parameters:

  • account – Account index

  • count – Number of addresses to generate

Returns:

List of address dicts

BIP39 mnemonic to seed implementation.

Minimal implementation for deriving a seed from a BIP39 mnemonic phrase.

ordex.wallet.bip39.mnemonic_to_seed(mnemonic: str, passphrase: str = '') → bytes[source]¶

Derive a seed from a BIP39 mnemonic phrase using PBKDF2.

Parameters:

  • mnemonic – BIP39 mnemonic phrase (12-24 words)

  • passphrase – Optional passphrase for extra security

Returns:

64-byte seed suitable for BIP32 HD wallet

Raises:

ValueError – If mnemonic is empty

Address generation for OrdexCoin and OrdexGold.

Supports P2PKH, P2SH, Bech32 (P2WPKH), and WIF encoding.

ordex.wallet.address.bech32_to_pubkey_hash(address: str) → Tuple[str, int, bytes][source]¶

Decode a Bech32/P2WPKH address.

Returns (hrp, witness_version, witness_program).

Raises ValueError if the address is invalid.

ordex.wallet.address.decode_address(address: str) → Dict[str, Any][source]¶

Decode any address type and return a dict with details.

Detects address type (P2PKH, P2SH, Bech32) and decodes accordingly.

Returns a dict with:

  • type: ‘p2pkh’, ‘p2sh’, ‘bech32’, or ‘unknown’

  • version: version byte (for Base58)

  • hash: the 20-byte hash (pubkey or script)

  • hrp: for Bech32 addresses

  • chain: detected chain hint based on prefix/HRP

ordex.wallet.address.generate_keypair(params: ChainParams) → Dict[str, Any][source]¶

Generate a new keypair and return (private_key, public_key, addresses).

Returns a dict with: - ‘privkey’: PrivateKey object - ‘pubkey’: PublicKey object - ‘wif’: WIF-encoded private key - ‘p2pkh’: P2PKH address - ‘p2sh’: P2SH address (P2PKH-in-P2SH) - ‘p2wpkh’: Bech32 P2WPKH address

ordex.wallet.address.p2pkh_to_pubkey_hash(address: str) → Tuple[bytes, bytes][source]¶

Decode a P2PKH address to extract the version byte and pubkey hash.

Returns (version_byte, pubkey_hash).

Raises ValueError if the address is invalid.

ordex.wallet.address.p2sh_to_script_hash(address: str) → Tuple[bytes, bytes][source]¶

Decode a P2SH address to extract the version byte and script hash.

Returns (version_byte, script_hash).

Raises ValueError if the address is invalid.

ordex.wallet.address.p2tr_to_pubkey_hash(address: str) → Tuple[str, bytes][source]¶

Decode a P2TR address.

Returns (hrp, witness_program).

ordex.wallet.address.privkey_to_wif(privkey: PrivateKey, params: ChainParams, compressed: bool = True) → str[source]¶

Encode a private key in WIF format with the chain’s SECRET_KEY prefix.

ordex.wallet.address.pubkey_to_bech32(pubkey: PublicKey, params: ChainParams) → str[source]¶

Generate a native SegWit P2WPKH address (Bech32).

Uses the chain’s bech32 HRP: OXC mainnet → ‘oxc1…’ OXG mainnet → ‘oxg1…’

ordex.wallet.address.pubkey_to_p2pkh(pubkey: PublicKey, params: ChainParams) → str[source]¶

Generate a P2PKH address from a public key.

Encodes as Base58Check with the chain’s PUBKEY_ADDRESS prefix. OXC mainnet → ‘X’ prefix (version 76) OXG mainnet → ‘G’ prefix (version 39)

ordex.wallet.address.pubkey_to_p2tr(pubkey: PublicKey, params: ChainParams) → str[source]¶

Generate a Taproot (P2TR) address from a public key.

Uses BIP341/342: Bech32m with witness version 1. The witness program is the SHA256 of the tweaked public key.

ordex.wallet.address.script_to_p2sh(redeem_script: bytes, params: ChainParams) → str[source]¶

Generate a P2SH address from a redeem script.

Encodes as Base58Check with the chain’s SCRIPT_ADDRESS prefix.

Transaction signing for OrdexCoin and OrdexGold.

Provides transaction signing functionality using ECDSA.

class ordex.wallet.signing.TransactionSignature(signature: bytes, sighash_type: int = 1)[source]¶

Represents a transaction input signature.

bytes() → bytes[source]¶

Return signature with sighash type appended.

ordex.wallet.signing.create_signed_transaction(inputs: List[tuple], outputs: List[tuple], private_key: PrivateKey, version: int = 2, locktime: int = 0) → CTransaction[source]¶

Create and sign a basic P2PKH transaction.

Parameters:

  • inputs – List of (txid, vout, amount) tuples for inputs

  • outputs – List of (address, amount) tuples for outputs

  • private_key – The key to sign with

  • version – Transaction version

  • locktime – Locktime

Returns:

Signed CTransaction

ordex.wallet.signing.sign_p2pkh_input(tx: CTransaction, input_index: int, private_key: PrivateKey, is_coinbase: bool = False) → None[source]¶

Sign a P2PKH input and update the transaction.

Parameters:

  • tx – The transaction to sign (modified in place)

  • input_index – Index of the input to sign

  • private_key – The private key to sign with

  • is_coinbase – Whether this is a coinbase input (skip signing)

ordex.wallet.signing.sign_transaction_input(tx: CTransaction, input_index: int, private_key: PrivateKey, script_code: bytes, sighash_type: int = 1) → bytes[source]¶

Sign a single transaction input.

Parameters:

  • tx – The transaction to sign

  • input_index – Index of the input to sign

  • private_key – The private key to sign with

  • script_code – The script being spent (for P2PKH: OP_DUP OP_HASH160 <pubkeyhash> OP_EQUALVERIFY OP_CHECKSIG)

  • sighash_type – Signature hash type (default: SIGHASH_ALL)

Returns:

The signature bytes with sighash type appended.

Chain Modules¶

Blockchain and consensus.

Full chain parameters for all OrdexCoin and OrdexGold networks.

Provides pre-configured ChainParams instances for mainnet, testnet, and regtest for both chains. All values extracted from the C++ source files: chainparams.cpp and kernel/chainparams.cpp.

class ordex.chain.chainparams.ChainParams(name: str = '', network_id: str = '', consensus: ConsensusParams = <factory>, message_start: bytes = b'\x00\x00\x00\x00', default_port: int = 0, protocol_version: int = 70016, prune_after_height: int = 0, pubkey_address_prefix: bytes = b'\x00', script_address_prefix: bytes = b'\x00', secret_key_prefix: bytes = b'\x00', ext_pubkey_prefix: bytes = b'\x00\x00\x00\x00', ext_secret_prefix: bytes = b'\x00\x00\x00\x00', bech32_hrp: str = '', mweb_hrp: str = '', genesis_block_hash: str = '', genesis_merkle_root: str = '', genesis_timestamp: str = 'Memento Mori', genesis_time: int = 0, genesis_nonce: int = 0, genesis_bits: int = 0, genesis_version: int = 1, genesis_reward: int = 0, genesis_output_script: bytes = b'', dns_seeds: List[str] = <factory>, checkpoints: dict = <factory>, coinbase_maturity: int = 100)[source]¶

Complete set of parameters defining a blockchain network.

ordex.chain.chainparams.get_chain_params(chain_id: str) → ChainParams[source]¶

Look up chain parameters by identifier.

Valid identifiers: oxc_main, oxc_test, oxc_regtest,

oxg_main, oxg_test, oxg_regtest.

ordex.chain.chainparams.oxc_mainnet() → ChainParams[source]¶

OrdexCoin mainnet parameters.

ordex.chain.chainparams.oxc_regtest() → ChainParams[source]¶

OrdexCoin regtest parameters.

ordex.chain.chainparams.oxc_testnet() → ChainParams[source]¶

OrdexCoin testnet parameters.

ordex.chain.chainparams.oxg_mainnet() → ChainParams[source]¶

OrdexGold mainnet parameters.

ordex.chain.chainparams.oxg_regtest() → ChainParams[source]¶

OrdexGold regtest parameters.

ordex.chain.chainparams.oxg_testnet() → ChainParams[source]¶

OrdexGold testnet parameters.

Block primitives for OrdexCoin and OrdexGold.

Implements CBlockHeader and CBlock with chain-aware PoW hash computation, Merkle tree calculation, and genesis block creation.

class ordex.primitives.block.CBlock(header: CBlockHeader | None = None, **kwargs)[source]¶

A full block: header + transaction list.

For OrdexGold, blocks may also contain an MWEB block body stored as opaque data (mweb_block_data).

check(params, check_pow: bool = True) → bool[source]¶

Validate the block.

Checks: - First transaction is coinbase - No duplicate transactions - Merkle root matches transactions - PoW (proof of work) - optional, enabled by default

Parameters:

  • params – ChainParams or ConsensusParams

  • check_pow – Whether to validate proof of work (default: True)

Returns:

True if block is valid

Raises:

ValueError – If validation fails

classmethod from_bytes(data: bytes, *, allow_mweb: bool = False) → CBlock[source]¶

Deserialize a block from bytes.

to_bytes(include_mweb: bool = True) → bytes[source]¶

Serialize the block to bytes.

class ordex.primitives.block.CBlockHeader(version: int = 0, hash_prev_block: bytes = b'\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00', hash_merkle_root: bytes = b'\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00', time: int = 0, bits: int = 0, nonce: int = 0)[source]¶

Standard 80-byte block header.

Fields:

version: Block version (int32) hash_prev_block: Hash of the previous block (32 bytes, internal order) hash_merkle_root: Merkle root of transactions (32 bytes) time: Block timestamp (uint32, Unix epoch) bits: Compact difficulty target (uint32) nonce: Mining nonce (uint32)

get_hash() → bytes[source]¶

Block identification hash — always SHA-256d for both chains.

This is the hash used for block IDs, prev_block references, etc.

get_hash_hex() → str[source]¶

Block hash as display hex (reversed byte order).

get_pow_hash(use_scrypt: bool = False) → bytes[source]¶

Proof-of-work hash.

Parameters:

use_scrypt – If True, use Scrypt (OrdexGold). Otherwise SHA-256d (OrdexCoin).

exception ordex.primitives.block.InvalidBlock[source]¶

Exception raised when block validation fails.

ordex.primitives.block.compute_merkle_root(tx_hashes: List[bytes]) → bytes[source]¶

Compute the Merkle root from a list of transaction hashes.

Each hash should be in internal byte order (as returned by sha256d). If the list has odd length, the last element is duplicated.

ordex.primitives.block.create_genesis_block(timestamp_str: str, genesis_output_script: bytes, ntime: int, nnonce: int, nbits: int, nversion: int, genesis_reward: int) → CBlock[source]¶

Create a genesis block matching the C++ CreateGenesisBlock() function.

Parameters:

  • timestamp_str – The coinbase script timestamp string (e.g. “Memento Mori”)

  • genesis_output_script – The output scriptPubKey (serialized CScript)

  • ntime – Block timestamp

  • nnonce – Block nonce

  • nbits – Compact difficulty target

  • nversion – Block version

  • genesis_reward – Coinbase reward in satoshis

Transaction primitives for OrdexCoin and OrdexGold.

Implements COutPoint, CTxIn, CTxOut, CTransaction, and CMutableTransaction with full SegWit serialization support. OrdexGold’s MWEB transaction flag (0x08) is handled by storing MWEB data as an opaque blob.

class ordex.primitives.transaction.COutPoint(hash: bytes = b'\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00', n: int = 4294967295)[source]¶

Reference to a specific output of a previous transaction.

class ordex.primitives.transaction.CTransaction(version: int = 2, vin: List[CTxIn] | None = None, vout: List[CTxOut] | None = None, locktime: int = 0, mweb_tx_data: bytes = b'', is_hogex: bool = False)[source]¶

An immutable Bitcoin/Ordex transaction with optional SegWit and MWEB data.

Serialization format:

Basic: [nVersion][vin][vout][nLockTime] SegWit: [nVersion][0x00][flags][vin][vout][witness…][nLockTime] MWEB (OXG): flag bit 0x08 indicates MWEB data follows witness

The MWEB transaction body is stored as opaque bytes (mweb_tx_data).

get_value_out() → int[source]¶

Sum of all output values.

txid() → bytes[source]¶

Transaction ID (hash without witness data, little-endian internal order).

txid_hex() → str[source]¶

Transaction ID as display hex (reversed byte order).

wtxid() → bytes[source]¶

Witness transaction ID (hash with witness data).

class ordex.primitives.transaction.CTxIn(prevout: COutPoint | None = None, script_sig: bytes = b'', sequence: int = 4294967295)[source]¶

Transaction input: references a previous output and provides a signature.

class ordex.primitives.transaction.CTxOut(value: int = -1, script_pubkey: bytes = b'')[source]¶

Transaction output: an amount and a locking script.

Consensus parameters.

Defines the ConsensusParams dataclass holding all consensus-critical configuration for a chain: PoW limits, halving schedule, BIP activation heights, etc.

class ordex.consensus.params.BIP9Deployment(bit: int = 28, start_time: int = -2, timeout: int = -2, min_activation_height: int = 0, start_height: int = 0, timeout_height: int = 0)[source]¶

BIP9 version-bits deployment configuration.

class ordex.consensus.params.ConsensusParams(hash_genesis_block: bytes = b'\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00', subsidy_halving_interval: int = 210000, bip16_height: int = 0, bip34_height: int = 0, bip34_hash: bytes = b'\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00', bip65_height: int = 0, bip66_height: int = 0, csv_height: int = 0, segwit_height: int = 0, min_bip9_warning_height: int = 0, rule_change_activation_threshold: int = 1916, miner_confirmation_window: int = 2016, deployments: Dict[str, ~ordex.consensus.params.BIP9Deployment]=<factory>, pow_limit: Uint256 = <factory>, pow_allow_min_difficulty_blocks: bool = False, pow_no_retargeting: bool = False, pow_target_spacing: int = 600, pow_target_timespan: int = 7200, minimum_chain_work: Uint256 = <factory>, default_assume_valid: Uint256 = <factory>, signet_blocks: bool = False, signet_challenge: bytes = b'', has_mweb: bool = False, use_scrypt: bool = False)[source]¶

All consensus-critical parameters for a chain.

property difficulty_adjustment_interval: int¶

Number of blocks between difficulty retargets.

Proof-of-work validation, difficulty retargeting, and block subsidy.

Implements the PoW rules for both OrdexCoin (Bitcoin-style) and OrdexGold (Litecoin-style with Art Forz fix and overflow shift).

ordex.consensus.pow.calculate_next_work_required_bitcoin(last_nbits: int, first_block_time: int, last_block_time: int, params: ConsensusParams) → int[source]¶

Standard Bitcoin/OXC difficulty retarget calculation.

Adjusts difficulty based on actual vs expected timespan, clamped to [timespan/4, timespan*4].

ordex.consensus.pow.calculate_next_work_required_litecoin(last_nbits: int, first_block_time: int, last_block_time: int, params: ConsensusParams) → int[source]¶

OrdexGold / Litecoin difficulty retarget with overflow fix.

Includes the intermediate uint256 overflow bit-shift guard (Art Forz fix).

ordex.consensus.pow.check_proof_of_work(pow_hash: bytes, nbits: int, params: ConsensusParams) → bool[source]¶

Validate that a PoW hash meets the claimed difficulty target.

Parameters:

  • pow_hash – The PoW hash (SHA-256d for OXC, Scrypt for OXG), internal byte order.

  • nbits – The compact target from the block header.

  • params – Consensus parameters (for pow_limit).

Returns:

True if the hash satisfies the target.

ordex.consensus.pow.get_block_subsidy(height: int, params: ConsensusParams) → int[source]¶

Calculate the block reward (subsidy) for a given height.

Both OXC and OXG use the same structure: - Block 1: large premine - All other blocks: standard reward - Reward halves every subsidy_halving_interval blocks.

Returns:

Block subsidy in satoshis.

Fee estimation for transactions.

Provides local fee estimation based on transaction size and historical fee data when RPC is unavailable.

class ordex.consensus.fee.FeeEstimate(feerate: float, estimate: str, blocks: int)[source]¶

Fee estimation result.

class ordex.consensus.fee.FeeEstimator(rpc_client=None)[source]¶

Local fee estimation based on mempool data and presets.

When connected to a node, can use RPC estimates. When offline, uses preset fee levels.

estimate_smart_fee(conf_target: int = 6, estimate_mode: str = 'conservative') → FeeEstimate[source]¶

Estimate fees for confirmation within target blocks.

Parameters:

  • conf_target – Target number of blocks for confirmation

  • estimate_mode – “conservative” or “economical”

Returns:

FeeEstimate with feerate in sat/vbyte

get_fee_for_transaction(tx_size: int, conf_target: int = 6) → int[source]¶

Calculate estimated fee for a transaction.

Parameters:

  • tx_size – Transaction size in bytes

  • conf_target – Target confirmation blocks

Returns:

Fee in satoshis

get_minimum_fee(tx_size: int) → int[source]¶

Get the minimum safe fee for a transaction.

Uses the network’s default minimum relay fee.

ordex.consensus.fee.calculate_fee_from_rate(tx_size: int, feerate: float) → int[source]¶

Calculate fee given transaction size and feerate.

Parameters:

  • tx_size – Transaction size in bytes

  • feerate – Fee rate in sat/vbyte

Returns:

Fee in satoshis

ordex.consensus.fee.calculate_vbytes_from_weight(weight: int) → int[source]¶

Calculate vsize (virtual bytes) from weight units.

Bitcoin uses weight units where: - Legacy bytes: 4 weight units - SegWit bytes: 1 weight unit vbytes = (weight + 3) // 4

Amount constants and validation for OrdexCoin and OrdexGold.

ordex.consensus.amount.format_money(value: int) → str[source]¶

Format a satoshi value as a human-readable coin amount.

ordex.consensus.amount.money_range(value: int, *, max_money: int = 845000000000000) → bool[source]¶

Check if a monetary value is within the valid range [0, MAX_MONEY].

ordex.consensus.amount.parse_money(s: str) → int[source]¶

Parse a coin amount string into satoshis.

Network Modules¶

P2P networking and RPC.

P2P message serialization / deserialization.

Implements the core Bitcoin P2P messages needed for handshake, ping/pong, block/transaction exchange, and header syncing.

class ordex.net.messages.CAddress(services: int = 0, ip: bytes = b'\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00', port: int = 0, timestamp: int | None = None)[source]¶

Network address (16 bytes IP + 2 bytes port + services).

class ordex.net.messages.MsgAddr(addresses: List[CAddress] | None = None)[source]¶

addr message — network addresses.

class ordex.net.messages.MsgGetData(inventory: List[CInv] | None = None)[source]¶

getdata message — request objects from a peer.

class ordex.net.messages.MsgGetHeaders(version: int = 70016, block_locator_hashes: List[bytes] | None = None, hash_stop: bytes = b'\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00')[source]¶

getheaders message — request block headers.

class ordex.net.messages.MsgHeaders(headers: List['CBlockHeader'] | None = None)[source]¶

headers message — response to getheaders, contains block headers.

class ordex.net.messages.MsgInv(inventory: List[CInv] | None = None)[source]¶

inv message — announce inventory.

class ordex.net.messages.MsgMempool[source]¶

mempool message — request transaction inventory.

class ordex.net.messages.MsgMerkleBlock(header: CBlockHeader = None, transaction_count: int = 0, hashes: List[bytes] | None = None, flags: bytes = b'')[source]¶

merkleblock message — filtered block with merkle proofs for SPV.

Contains a block header plus a partial merkle tree showing which transactions match the bloom filter.

class ordex.net.messages.MsgPing(nonce: int | None = None)[source]¶

ping message.

class ordex.net.messages.MsgPong(nonce: int = 0)[source]¶

pong message — response to ping.

class ordex.net.messages.MsgVersion(version: int = 70016, services: int = <ServiceFlags.NODE_NETWORK|NODE_WITNESS: 9>, timestamp: int | None = None, user_agent: str = '/ordex-python:0.1.0/', start_height: int = 0, relay: bool = True)[source]¶

version message — initiates the P2P handshake.

Block synchronization logic for P2P networking.

Provides header download, block locator construction, and chain state management for initial block download (IBD).

class ordex.net.sync.BlockSynchronizer(conn: NodeConnection, params: ChainParams | None = None)[source]¶

Handles header and block synchronization with peers.

Usage:

sync = BlockSynchronizer(conn, chain_params)
await sync.download_headers(peer_height=800000)
await sync.download_blocks()
async download_blocks(start_hash: bytes | None = None, max_blocks: int = 16) → List[bytes][source]¶

Download blocks starting from a given hash.

Parameters:

  • start_hash – Hash to start from (default: tip)

  • max_blocks – Maximum blocks to download

Returns:

List of serialized blocks

async download_headers(peer_height: int = 0, max_headers: int = 2000) → List[CBlockHeader][source]¶

Download block headers from peer.

Parameters:

  • peer_height – Known height of peer’s chain tip

  • max_headers – Maximum headers to request

Returns:

List of downloaded headers

async get_peer_inventory() → List[CInv][source]¶

Request mempool inventory from peer.

async request_block(block_hash: bytes) → bytes | None[source]¶

Request a specific block from the peer.

Returns the serialized block or None if not received.

class ordex.net.sync.ChainState(params: ChainParams)[source]¶

Tracks the local chain state during sync.

add_header(header: CBlockHeader) → bool[source]¶

Add a header to the chain state.

Returns True if this is a new header.

find_gap(headers: List[CBlockHeader]) → int | None[source]¶

Find the first gap in the header chain.

Returns the index where the gap starts, or None if contiguous.

get_locator() → List[bytes][source]¶

Build a block locator for getheaders message.

class ordex.net.sync.PeerManager(params: ChainParams)[source]¶

Manages connections to multiple peers and tracks their chain state.

async add_peer(host: str, port: int) → bool[source]¶

Connect to a new peer and perform handshake.

Returns True if connection successful.

async close_all() → None[source]¶

Close all peer connections.

async disconnect_peer(host: str, port: int) → None[source]¶

Disconnect from a peer.

Async P2P node connection handler.

Manages TCP connections to ordexcoind / ordexgoldd nodes, performs the version handshake, and provides send/receive primitives.

class ordex.net.connection.NodeConnection(host: str, port: int, chain_params: ChainParams)[source]¶

Async TCP connection to a P2P node.

Usage:

conn = NodeConnection("127.0.0.1", 25174, chain_params)
await conn.connect()
await conn.handshake()
# ... send/receive messages
await conn.close()
async close() → None[source]¶

Close the connection.

async connect(timeout: float = 10.0) → None[source]¶

Establish the TCP connection.

async handshake(start_height: int = 0) → None[source]¶

Perform the version/verack handshake.

async listen(timeout: float = 60.0) → None[source]¶

Listen for incoming messages and dispatch to handlers.

Runs until timeout or connection close.

async recv_message(timeout: float = 30.0) → tuple[str, bytes][source]¶

Receive a framed P2P message.

Returns (command, payload).

register_handler(command: str, handler: Callable) → None[source]¶

Register a callback for a specific message type.

async send_message(command: str, payload: bytes) → None[source]¶

Send a framed P2P message.

P2P protocol definitions: message header, inventory vectors, service flags.

Matches the on-wire format used by ordexcoind / ordexgoldd.

class ordex.net.protocol.CInv(inv_type: int = 0, hash: bytes = b'\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00')[source]¶

Inventory vector: type + hash.

class ordex.net.protocol.CMessageHeader(message_start: bytes = b'\x00\x00\x00\x00', command: str = '', payload_size: int = 0, checksum: bytes = b'\x00\x00\x00\x00')[source]¶

P2P message header (24 bytes total).

Layout:

[4] message start (magic bytes) [12] command (null-padded ASCII) [4] payload size (uint32 LE) [4] checksum (first 4 bytes of SHA-256d of payload)

classmethod from_payload(message_start: bytes, command: str, payload: bytes) → CMessageHeader[source]¶

Create a header for a given command and payload.

verify_checksum(payload: bytes) → bool[source]¶

Verify the checksum against the actual payload.

class ordex.net.protocol.InvType(*values)[source]¶
class ordex.net.protocol.ServiceFlags(*values)[source]¶

JSON-RPC client for ordexcoind / ordexgoldd.

Provides a clean Python interface to the full node’s RPC API.

class ordex.rpc.client.RpcClient(url: str = 'http://127.0.0.1:25175', username: str = 'rpcuser', password: str = 'rpcpass', timeout: int = 30)[source]¶

JSON-RPC 1.0 client for ordexcoind / ordexgoldd.

Usage:

rpc = RpcClient("http://127.0.0.1:25175", "rpcuser", "rpcpass")
info = rpc.getblockchaininfo()
print(info["blocks"])
call(method: str, *params: Any) → Any[source]¶

Make a JSON-RPC call.

Parameters:

  • method – The RPC method name.

  • *params – Positional parameters.

Returns:

The result field from the RPC response.

Raises:

  • RpcError – If the RPC server returns an error.

  • requests.RequestException – On network errors.

exception ordex.rpc.client.RpcError(code: int, message: str)[source]¶

Error returned by the RPC server.

Ordex Python Library

Navigation

Related Topics

  • Documentation overview
